title: Masala Omelette Pav

subs.name: besan (gram flour) batter for a vegan masala 'omelette' — see besan chilla
subs.notes: use 0.5 cup besan whisked with water and the same spices

subs.name: whole-wheat roti or sandwich bread if pav is unavailable
subs.notes: pav buns are ideally soft and slightly sweet

steps.text: Crack 2 eggs into a bowl (for one portion). Add half the onion, tomato, green chilli and coriander leaves. Sprinkle in turmeric, chilli powder, black pepper and salt. Beat together with a fork until everything is well combined — this is your masala egg mixture for one omelette.

Heat 1 tsp oil in a tawa or flat pan on medium-high flame until shimmering. Pour in the egg mixture and spread it into a round. Let it cook undisturbed for about 1 minute until the edges firm up and the bottom is set but the top is still slightly runny.

Fold the omelette in half, press gently, and cook for another 30 seconds per side. The centre should be cooked through but still moist. Slide onto a plate. Repeat with the remaining mixture for the second omelette.

While the omelettes are cooking, toast the pav buns cut-side down in butter on the same tawa for 30–60 seconds until golden. Tuck one omelette into each pair of pav buns. Serve immediately — the combination of hot omelette and buttered pav is the whole point.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
critical_checks: Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: egg → besan (gram flour) batter for a vegan masala 'omelette' — see besan chilla (use 0.5 cup besan whisked with water and the same spices); bread → whole-wheat roti or sandwich bread if pav is unavailable (pav buns are ideally soft and slightly sweet); butter → oil for a dairy-free finish
